steps.title: Apply the corresponding angles property with parallel lines
steps.content: BD is parallel to AE, so line CE is a transversal cutting both parallel lines. Angles AEC and BDC are corresponding angles in this configuration. By the corresponding angles property: $$\angle AEC = \angle BDC = 58°$$

steps.title: Find angle CAE using the triangle angle sum
steps.content: In triangle ACE, the angles sum to 180°. We know $\angle AEC = 58°$ and $\angle ACE = 62°$, so: $$\angle CAE + 62° + 58° = 180°$$ $$\angle CAE = 60°$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
